markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Ingest operator-created research notes as context with explicit non-normative authority labels unless promoted by spec enrichment. This MT belongs to SourceIngestionAndEvidence. Ingest project, governance, media, and research sources with evidence and failure receipts.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Source ingestion must be allowlisted, hash-based, restartable, and secret-aware.
PDF/media extraction failures must produce receipts and repairable metadata rather than silently indexing partial content.
scope.expected_behavior: OperatorResearchNoteIngestion leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: OperatorResearchNoteIngestion is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

lifecycle.implementation_evidence: wtc-kernel-009 feat/WP-KERNEL-009 d0393ffc; real-PG tests green (group total 80 passed/0 failed; lopdf 0.41 MIT only dep added). Pending adversarial code review before READY_FOR_VALIDATION.

concerns: Authority labelling correct: engine.rs stamps provenance.authority = 'non_normative_context' for OperatorResearchNote kind (verified pipeline test asserts it). Notes are context, not authority, until a spec enrichment promotes them — matches MT-090 contract and [GLOBAL-GOVARTIFACTS-028].

Fenced code blocks (``` ... ```) stay single spans and a heading-looking line INSIDE a fence ('# not a heading') never enters the heading path — guards against markdown-injection of fake section structure. Heading stack pop logic is level-correct.

Wikilinks recorded as CANDIDATES only (never resolved at ingestion) — link_candidates ride along as JSONB for later graph work. detect_wikilinks handles malformed/unclosed/nested [[ without panic (spans.rs).
Notes pass redact_spans (secret_scan=true): a credential pasted into a research note is redacted/blocked. Empty/whitespace-only note yields zero spans (no empty rows).
evasion_probes: heading path tracking across #/## levels CONFIRMED (pipeline mt085_mt090 + notes unit tests).
fenced code block with fake heading stays one block, fake heading excluded from path CONFIRMED.
wikilink [[target]] and [[target|label]] -> candidates with target/label CONFIRMED.
malformed [[unclosed / [[Nested[[x]] handled without panic CONFIRMED (spans.rs wikilink test).
evidence_checked: engine.rs:367-371 provenance authority stamp; engine.rs:1038-1053 note branch

evidence: Adversarial code review PASS/PASS_WITH_NOTES (ADVERSARIAL_CODE_REVIEWER-20260611-PM); only LOW non-blocking notes.

wp_validator_review_v1.review_scope: Validated only MTs that were READY_FOR_VALIDATION in WP-KERNEL-009; no ACP used.

findings.summary: MT-090 is READY_FOR_VALIDATION but declares MT-081 as a dependency, and MT-081 is still CLAIMED rather than validator-completed.
findings.required_remediation: Do not resubmit MT-090 until MT-081 is READY_FOR_VALIDATION and passes WP Validator review, or update the dependency graph through the packet authority if the dependency is no longer real.

evidence_basis: Authoritative MT JSON contract and current product tree inspection.
Dependency failures use deterministic packet status/dependency reads only; WIP MT work products were not inspected.
remediation_summary: Do not resubmit MT-090 until MT-081 is READY_FOR_VALIDATION and passes WP Validator review, or update the dependency graph through the packet authority if the dependency is no longer real.

remediation_v2.fail_verdict: FAIL_V1 BLOCKED_ON_DEPENDENCY (MT-081 unfinished at validation time)
remediation_v2.remediation: Dependency resolved: MT-081 is now implemented+hardened+verified (commit 5876feaf) and READY_FOR_VALIDATION. This MT own implementation passed adversarial review; the only V1 blocker was the MT-081 dependency. Resubmitting for V2 re-validation.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-090 requires ingesting operator research notes as non-normative context: split markdown/text into heading-path-anchored paragraph spans, record [[wikilink]] candidates per span (never resolved at ingestion), and stamp provenance authority=non_normative_context, with durable PostgreSQL/EventLedger proof. The code (notes.rs parser + engine.rs integration) implements exactly this and is proven by a real Handshake-managed PostgreSQL pipeline test asserting persisted spans, heading paths, wikilink candidates, the non_normative_context stamp, and a replayable ledger event.
findings: notes.rs:52-174 parse_note() is a real, complete parser: paragraph blocks split on blank lines, fenced code blocks (```) kept as single spans with in_fence tracking, heading stack with correct level-pop semantics (l>=level pop), 1-based line anchors, heading_path carried per span. No placeholders/todo!/unimplemented! anywhere (grep clean).

Heading-injection guard verified: notes.rs:84-103 routes fence lines so a '# not a heading' line inside a fence never reaches heading_level(); unit test fenced_code_blocks_stay_single_spans (notes.rs:236-257) and line 109 heading path both assert the fake heading is excluded.

engine.rs:1071-1086 wires OperatorResearchNote (and MarkdownText) kind to parse_note() in the real extraction path, producing persisted spans + error_detail rollup (headings_seen/link_candidates). engine.rs:369-372 stamps provenance['authority']=non_normative_context for OperatorResearchNote kind, satisfying the context-not-authority requirement and [GLOBAL-GOVARTIFACTS-028].

sub_rule_2 (real resource touch) PASS: tests/knowledge_ingestion_support.rs:1-39 builds on knowledge_pg() = real Handshake-managed PostgreSQL with per-test isolated schema and full migration chain, explicitly 'no SQLite, no mocks'; PostgresDatabase::connect feeds IngestionEngine::from_database. The MT-090 test (knowledge_ingestion_pipeline_tests.rs:110-196) ingests a note through the real engine and asserts durable spans, heading_path=['Research'], 2 wikilink candidates (target/label) read from persisted JSONB, provenance authority=non_normative_context, and a real kernel_event_ledger row queried via sqlx carrying actor/session/correlation + extraction_receipt payload, plus per-source rollup (last_index_receipt_event_id). This is real-PG end-to-end proof, not implementer-authored mocks.

sub_rule_1 (no deferred-live escape) PASS: grep of notes.rs and the engine note branch for todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/'not yet wired'/'deferred to follow'/'pending MT'/Unavailable returns no hits on the live path.

Wikilink robustness: spans.rs detect_wikilinks handles malformed/empty/nested/newline cases ([[]], [[a\nb]] -> none) per its tests; candidates are recorded only, never resolved at ingestion, matching MT scope and the link_candidates JSONB serialization the PG test reads.

Prior FAIL_V1 (wp_validator_review_v1) was UPSTREAM_DEPENDENCY_NOT_READY (MT-081 still CLAIMED at validation time) — a sequencing/paperwork concern, not a code defect; remediation_v2 records MT-081 now READY_FOR_VALIDATION (commit 5876feaf). Per ground rules this dependency-ordering issue is not a code failing reason and the implementation itself was never faulted.
Aside (non-blocking, paperwork): MT contract cites spec bundle v02.192 anchors; current is v02.194 with the same section numbers (2.3.13.11). Stale spec pointer is expected and not a failing reason.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
